translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The song of songs, which is Solomon’s.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth: for thy love is better than wine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because of the savour of thy good ointments thy name is as ointment poured forth, therefore do the virgins love thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Draw me, we will run after thee: the king hath brought me into his chambers: we will be glad and rejoice in thee, we will remember thy love more than wine: the upright love thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am black, but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, as the tents of Kedar, as the curtains of Solomon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Look not upon me, because I am black, because the sun hath looked upon me: my mother’s children were angry with me; they made me the keeper of the vineyards; but mine own vineyard have I not kept.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Tell me, O thou whom my soul loveth, where thou feedest, where thou makest thy flock to rest at noon: for why should I be as one that turneth aside by the flocks of thy companions?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou know not, O thou fairest among women, go thy way forth by the footsteps of the flock, and feed thy kids beside the shepherds’ tents.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have compared thee, O my love, to a company of horses in Pharaoh’s chariots.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy cheeks are comely with rows of jewels, thy neck with chains of gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: We will make thee borders of gold with studs of silver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: While the king sitteth at his table, my spikenard sendeth forth the smell thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A bundle of myrrh is my well-beloved unto me; he shall lie all night betwixt my breasts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved is unto me as a cluster of camphire in the vineyards of Engedi.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves’ eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, thou art fair, my beloved, yea, pleasant: also our bed is green.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The beams of our house are cedar, and our rafters of fir.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am the rose of Sharon, and the lily of the valleys.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the lily among thorns, so is my love among the daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so is my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit was sweet to my taste.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me was love.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Stay me with flagons, comfort me with apples: for I am sick of love.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The voice of my beloved! behold, he cometh leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved spake, and said unto me, Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For, lo, the winter is past, the rain is over and gone;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The flowers appear on the earth; the time of the singing of birds is come, and the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fig tree putteth forth her green figs, and the vines with the tender grape give a good smell. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O my dove, that art in the clefts of the rock, in the secret places of the stairs, let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; for sweet is thy voice, and thy countenance is comely.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vines: for our vines have tender grapes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved is mine, and I am his: he feedeth among the lilies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be thou like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will rise now, and go about the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I will seek him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The watchmen that go about the city found me: to whom I said, Saw ye him whom my soul loveth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It was but a little that I passed from them, but I found him whom my soul loveth: I held him, and would not let him go, until I had brought him into my mother’s house, and into the chamber of her that conceived me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is this that cometh out of the wilderness like pillars of smoke, perfumed with myrrh and frankincense, with all powders of the merchant?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold his bed, which is Solomon’s; threescore valiant men are about it, of the valiant of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They all hold swords, being expert in war: every man hath his sword upon his thigh because of fear in the night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: King Solomon made himself a chariot of the wood of Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He made the pillars thereof of silver, the bottom thereof of gold, the covering of it of purple, the midst thereof being paved with love, for the daughters of Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go forth, O ye daughters of Zion, and behold king Solomon with the crown wherewith his mother crowned him in the day of his espousals, and in the day of the gladness of his heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves’ eyes within thy locks: thy hair is as a flock of goats, that appear from mount Gilead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy teeth are like a flock of sheep that are even shorn, which came up from the washing; whereof every one bear twins, and none is barren among them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy lips are like a thread of scarlet, and thy speech is comely: thy temples are like a piece of a pomegranate within thy locks.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy neck is like the tower of David builded for an armoury, whereon there hang a thousand bucklers, all shields of mighty men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins, which feed among the lilies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou art all fair, my love; there is no spot in thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Come with me from Lebanon, my spouse, with me from Lebanon: look from the top of Amana, from the top of Shenir and Hermon, from the lions’ dens, from the mountains of the leopards.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse; thou hast ravished my heart with one of thine eyes, with one chain of thy neck.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How fair is thy love, my sister, my spouse! how much better is thy love than wine! and the smell of thine ointments than all spices!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy lips, O my spouse, drop as the honeycomb: honey and milk are under thy tongue; and the smell of thy garments is like the smell of Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A garden inclosed is my sister, my spouse; a spring shut up, a fountain sealed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy plants are an orchard of pomegranates, with pleasant fruits; camphire, with spikenard,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Spikenard and saffron; calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense; myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, and streams from Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Awake, O north wind; and come, thou south; blow upon my garden, that the spices thereof may flow out. Let my beloved come into his garden, and eat his pleasant fruits.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am come into my garden, my sister, my spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I sleep, but my heart waketh: it is the voice of my beloved that knocketh, saying, Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is filled with dew, and my locks with the drops of the night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have put off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved put in his hand by the hole of the door, and my bowels were moved for him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I rose up to open to my beloved; and my hands dropped with myrrh, and my fingers with sweet smelling myrrh, upon the handles of the lock.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, and was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The watchmen that went about the city found me, they smote me, they wounded me; the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I am sick of love.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What is thy beloved more than another beloved, O thou fairest among women? what is thy beloved more than another beloved, that thou dost so charge us?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved is white and ruddy, the chiefest among ten thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His head is as the most fine gold, his locks are bushy, and black as a raven.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, and fitly set.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His cheeks are as a bed of spices, as sweet flowers: his lips like lilies, dropping sweet smelling myrrh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His hands are as gold rings set with the beryl: his belly is as bright ivory overlaid with sapphires.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His legs are as pillars of marble, set upon sockets of fine gold: his countenance is as Lebanon, excellent as the cedars.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His mouth is most sweet: yea, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside? that we may seek him with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the beds of spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am my beloved’s, and my beloved is mine: he feedeth among the lilies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou art beautiful, O my love, as Tirzah, comely as Jerusalem, terrible as an army with banners.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Turn away thine eyes from me, for they have overcome me: thy hair is as a flock of goats that appear from Gilead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy teeth are as a flock of sheep which go up from the washing, whereof every one beareth twins, and there is not one barren among them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a piece of a pomegranate are thy temples within thy locks.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There are threescore queens, and fourscore concubines, and virgins without number.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My dove, my undefiled is but one; she is the only one of her mother, she is the choice one of her that bare her. The daughters saw her, and blessed her; yea, the queens and the concubines, and they praised her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is she that looketh forth as the morning, fair as the moon, clear as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I went down into the garden of nuts to see the fruits of the valley, and to see whether the vine flourished, and the pomegranates budded.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or ever I was aware, my soul made me like the chariots of Amminadib.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Return, return, O Shulamite; return, return, that we may look upon thee. What will ye see in the Shulamite? As it were the company of two armies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How beautiful are thy feet with shoes, O prince’s daughter! the joints of thy thighs are like jewels, the work of the hands of a cunning workman.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy navel is like a round goblet, which wanteth not liquor: thy belly is like an heap of wheat set about with lilies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy neck is as a tower of ivory; thine eyes like the fishpools in Heshbon, by the gate of Bathrabbim: thy nose is as the tower of Lebanon which looketh toward Damascus.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thine head upon thee is like Carmel, and the hair of thine head like purple; the king is held in the galleries.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This thy stature is like to a palm tree, and thy breasts to clusters of grapes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I said, I will go up to the palm tree, I will take hold of the boughs thereof: now also thy breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the smell of thy nose like apples;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the roof of thy mouth like the best wine for my beloved, that goeth down sweetly, causing the lips of those that are asleep to speak.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am my beloved’s, and his desire is toward me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Come, my beloved, let us go forth into the field; let us lodge in the villages.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see if the vine flourish, whether the tender grape appear, and the pomegranates bud forth: there will I give thee my loves.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The mandrakes give a smell, and at our gates are all manner of pleasant fruits, new and old, which I have laid up for thee, O my beloved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O that thou wert as my brother, that sucked the breasts of my mother! when I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; yea, I should not be despised.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I would lead thee, and bring thee into my mother’s house, who would instruct me: I would cause thee to drink of spiced wine of the juice of my pomegranate.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His left hand should be under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, until he please.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved? I raised thee up under the apple tree: there thy mother brought thee forth: there she brought thee forth that bare thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death; jealousy is cruel as the grave: the coals thereof are coals of fire, which hath a most vehement flame.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Many waters cannot quench love, neither can the floods drown it: if a man would give all the substance of his house for love, it would utterly be contemned.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: We have a little sister, and she hath no breasts: what shall we do for our sister in the day when she shall be spoken for?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If she be a wall, we will build upon her a palace of silver: and if she be a door, we will inclose her with boards of cedar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am a wall, and my breasts like towers: then was I in his eyes as one that found favour.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Solomon had a vineyard at Baalhamon; he let out the vineyard unto keepers; every one for the fruit thereof was to bring a thousand pieces of silver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My vineyard, which is mine, is before me: thou, O Solomon, must have a thousand, and those that keep the fruit thereof two hundred.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken to thy voice: cause me to hear it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a roe or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices.
